master_phase_name: Phase 20 — Privacy / Export / Memory Control / External Connector Trust Layer
rule: If a Phase 20 feature is not written into this lock, memory is not trusted to remember it later.
purpose: Lock every Phase 20 feature, step, build chunk, cross-phase connection, safety rule, source coverage note, Spotify account connection boundary, and connector trust boundary before implementation continues.

source_coverage_carryover.decision: No major original DOCX or JSON gap blocks Phase 20. New Phase 20 additions must be locked here before implementation.

full_feature_inventory: forget versus hide versus archive versus destructive action definitions
manual workout logging through Carnos chat
manual workout logging through Carnos voice later
final v1 polish/deployment/demo polish excluded because that is Phase 21
total_steps: Clean temporary audit files and confirm repo is clean.
Record all minor old-source notes and all new Phase 20 additions.
Lock all 20A-20Z build chunks inside the repo.
Add a 20A audit that fails if any Phase 20 feature/chunk is missing from the lock.
Define Private Mode and Timed Private Mode.
Define Sensitive Locks and domain privacy permissions.
Define two-step confirmation and cooldown boundary.
Define export contracts, export manifest, and export expiration.
Define forget/destructive action manifest and safety boundary.
Define Audit Viewer and append-only audit boundary.
Define privacy audit taxonomy, redaction rules, and privacy badges.
Define connector registry, account model, permissions, scopes, token boundary, action review, audit events.
Define Spotify permissions, scopes, and risk levels.
Define Spotify playback, current-track, device, playlist, and recently-played boundaries.
Define Spotify Carnos actions and action safety.
Define Spotify action manifest and audit events.
Define manual workout logging through Carnos chat/voice.
Define explicit exclusions and deferred items.
Add Phase 20 fixtures covering memory, privacy, export, destructive, connector, and Spotify states.
Add package check integration for Phase 20 audits.

chunks.name: Scope lock + coverage carryover + source interconnection map
chunks.purpose: Create the repo-side Phase 20 constitution so no Phase 20 feature, step, chunk, Spotify requirement, connector requirement, exclusion, deferral, source note, or safety rule can be forgotten later.
does: locks every Phase 20 feature before implementation
locks what every chunk builds and does
records acceptance and not-allowed rules for every chunk
makes the repo the source of memory for Phase 20
prevents chat drift and terminal drift
acceptance: all 20A through 20Z chunks exist in the fixture and roadmap
each chunk has purpose, builds, does, acceptance, not_allowed, and feature_markers
not_allowed: do not implement runtime Spotify OAuth yet
do not create live connector execution yet
do not add real destructive action execution
do not skip any Phase 20 feature from the lock
do not rely on chat memory after this chunk

chunks.name: Core privacy domain contracts
chunks.purpose: Define the primitive privacy language used by every later Phase 20 feature.
does: prevents later features from inventing conflicting states
supports memory, export, destructive actions, connectors, and Spotify
not_allowed: do not add UI yet
do not add live DB writes
do not make Carnos bypass rules

chunks.name: Memory control contracts
chunks.purpose: Upgrade memory into user-governed memory control with Memory Inbox and Saved Memories.
does: lets user review proposed memories before saving
shows what Carnos can remember and use
acceptance: approval, rejection, and deferral are defined
silent sensitive memory save is blocked
not_allowed: do not allow Carnos to approve its own memory
do not silently save sensitive memory
do not allow rejected memory in Carnos context

chunks.name: Forget / hide / archive / destructive action semantics
chunks.purpose: Define the difference between forget, hide, archive, and protected destructive actions.
does: defines what Carnos can no longer use
defines what dashboards should no longer show
defines what remains for proof and audit
acceptance: forget is defined as removing from Carnos use
hide is defined as removing from normal surfaces
archive is defined as retaining proof but removing active use
destructive action is defined as protected high-risk removal
not_allowed: do not confuse forget with destructive action
do not allow Carnos destructive action
do not remove audit history casually

chunks.name: Private Mode + Timed Private Mode
chunks.purpose: Make Private Mode an enforceable ruleset across memory, dashboards, analytics, connectors, and Spotify.
does: records enable, disable, and expiry events
acceptance: connector and Spotify restrictions are defined
not_allowed: do not make Private Mode a vague toggle
do not allow silent memory creation during Private Mode
do not allow Spotify private-mode bypass

chunks.name: Emergency Lockdown
chunks.purpose: Add stronger privacy protection than Private Mode for sensitive situations.
not_allowed: do not let Carnos disable lockdown
do not allow sensitive exports by default during lockdown
do not allow connector or Spotify bypass

chunks.name: Sensitive Locks + domain privacy permissions
chunks.purpose: Make privacy domain-aware across every major ascendOS area.
does: locks health, body, finance, documents, memory, custom trackers, analytics, external connectors, and Spotify data
acceptance: Spotify data is included as a sensitive domain
not_allowed: do not let locked data leak into dashboards
do not let locked data leak into analytics
do not let Carnos read fully locked domains

chunks.name: Carnos access matrix
chunks.purpose: Define exactly what Carnos can read, summarize, suggest, remember, expose, or propose.
does: blocks Carnos from raw token access
blocks Carnos from export and destructive actions
allows only approved safe action execution boundaries
acceptance: connector and Spotify restrictions are included
not_allowed: do not allow Carnos raw token access
do not allow Carnos silent export
do not allow Carnos destructive action
do not allow Carnos silent Spotify action

chunks.name: Privacy action review queue
chunks.purpose: Centralize review for privacy, memory, export, destructive, connector, and Spotify actions.
does: prevents dangerous actions from executing silently
not_allowed: do not allow unreviewed high-risk actions
do not allow Carnos to approve its own action
do not skip audit links

chunks.name: Two-step confirmation + cooldown boundary
chunks.purpose: Protect high-risk actions with stronger confirmation and waiting boundaries.
does: protects Spotify device and volume control
not_allowed: do not allow high-risk actions without confirmation
do not allow expired actions to execute
do not bypass cooldown

chunks.name: Data scope selector
chunks.purpose: Create one shared scope model for export, forget, hide, archive, destructive actions, connectors, and Spotify previews.
does: makes export and destructive actions explainable
acceptance: custom tracker and evidence scopes are included
not_allowed: do not allow unscoped export
do not allow unscoped destructive action
do not skip sensitive and privacy filters

chunks.name: Export contracts + export manifest + expiration
chunks.purpose: Make exports scoped, previewed, manifested, expiring, and audited.
not_allowed: do not allow Carnos export
do not allow export all without stronger confirmation
do not include locked data without warning or explicit approval

chunks.name: Forget/destructive manifest + destructive safety
chunks.purpose: Make forget and protected destructive actions previewable, confirmable, manifested, and audited.
does: prevents Carnos from performing destructive actions
not_allowed: do not allow Carnos destructive action
do not allow high-risk removal without manifest
do not skip audit

chunks.name: Audit Viewer + append-only audit boundary
chunks.purpose: Make privacy trust visible and tamper-resistant.
does: shows privacy, memory, export, connector, and Spotify events
links events to manifests and sources
acceptance: connector and Spotify audit events are supported
not_allowed: do not allow Carnos audit edits
do not allow silent audit removal
do not expose sensitive payloads without redaction

chunks.name: Privacy audit taxonomy + redaction + badges
chunks.purpose: Standardize privacy event names, redaction behavior, and UI badges.
does: shows privacy state clearly in UI
supports redacted display across dashboard, export, memory, analytics, connectors, and Spotify
not_allowed: do not use inconsistent event names
do not expose sensitive fields without redaction
do not skip Spotify badges

chunks.name: Data retention boundary
chunks.purpose: Define how long privacy artifacts remain active before review, archive, or expiry.
not_allowed: do not keep active exports forever
do not keep action approvals valid forever
do not skip audit for retention changes

chunks.name: Cross-phase privacy enforcement map
chunks.purpose: Prove Phase 20 is connected to earlier phases and not a standalone page.
does: connects privacy enforcement to custom trackers
connects privacy enforcement to current info
connects privacy enforcement to connectors and Spotify
acceptance: all prior phase links are recorded
not_allowed: do not make Phase 20 standalone
do not ignore Phase 19 custom tracker privacy
do not ignore Phase 18 analytics privacy

chunks.name: Future API Connector Framework
chunks.purpose: Create the reusable trust layer for present Spotify and later external APIs.
does: creates a common model for future APIs
supports Spotify as the first connector
not_allowed: do not build every future connector now
do not expose raw tokens
do not allow Carnos to connect APIs by itself
do not allow silent external actions

chunks.name: Spotify connector foundation
chunks.purpose: Make Spotify the first real connector inside the Phase 20 trust layer.
does: records scope grants and missing scopes
not_allowed: do not ask user for Spotify password
do not expose raw Spotify tokens
do not allow Carnos token access
do not execute Spotify actions silently

chunks.name: Spotify account connection + OAuth/PKCE callback boundary
chunks.purpose: Define exactly how the user connects a Spotify account safely.
does: keeps Carnos away from raw tokens
not_allowed: do not ask user for password
do not log tokens
do not store tokens in memory
do not expose token values to Carnos
do not connect without user OAuth

chunks.name: Spotify playback/device/playlist/recently-played boundaries
chunks.purpose: Safely model Spotify read-side data without leaking sensitive listening history.
does: blocks reads when scopes are missing
acceptance: recently played sensitive boundary is defined
not_allowed: do not convert listening history into memory without approval
do not expose sensitive Spotify data during Private Mode
do not ignore missing scopes

chunks.name: Spotify Carnos actions + action safety
chunks.purpose: Allow Carnos to propose Spotify actions while preventing silent media control.
does: blocks action when private mode is active
blocks action when scope is missing
blocks action when Premium or device requirements fail
acceptance: Spotify action proposal model is defined
no silent Spotify action guarantee exists
not_allowed: do not allow Carnos to play music silently
do not allow volume or device control without approval
do not bypass private mode
do not ignore missing scopes

chunks.name: Spotify UI + Media Permissions + Connector Audit cards
chunks.purpose: Make Spotify and connector controls visible on /privacy.
does: shows connector and Spotify audit summaries
not_allowed: do not build unsafe live action buttons without review boundary
do not hide missing scope warnings
do not hide Premium-required warnings

chunks.name: Manual workout logging stance + exclusion/deferred registry
chunks.purpose: Lock workout logging through Carnos and prevent accidental connector sprawl.
builds: manual workout logging through Carnos chat
manual workout logging through Carnos voice later
does: keeps workout logging manual through Carnos for now
connects workout logs to custom trackers and health/body systems
explicitly defers Garmin and other connectors
acceptance: structured workout proposal fields are recorded
not_allowed: do not build Garmin in Phase 20
do not build Echo in Phase 20
do not build all future connectors in Phase 20
do not skip Carnos review-before-save
feature_markers: manual workout logging through Carnos chat

chunks.name: /privacy dashboard view model + UI
chunks.purpose: Upgrade /privacy into the visible Phase 20 command center.
does: turns Phase 20 contracts into UI-ready state
shows all privacy and connector controls
shows blocked, expired, missing scope, Premium-required, and no-active-device states
acceptance: no unsafe runtime action is introduced
not_allowed: do not add fake live destructive actions
do not add unsafe Spotify execution
do not omit any locked card
do not use hardcoded demo data as final state

chunks.name: Final Phase 20 proof + package check integration + completion
chunks.purpose: Prove Phase 20 complete, safe, checked, committed, and ready for Phase 21.
not_allowed: do not declare Phase 20 complete without full check
do not skip completion report
do not start Phase 21 before Phase 20Z is committed

enforcement_requirements: custom tracker privacy respects sensitive locks
spotify_account_connection_flow: Spotify redirects back to ascendOS callback route.
ascendOS validates state and PKCE boundary.
Carnos can see connection status and allowed permissions.
spotify_required_user_setup: Use local redirect URI for development.
spotify_carnos_actions: suggest playlist based on focus session
exclusions_and_deferrals: final v1 polish/deployment/demo polish excluded because that is Phase 21
safety_guarantees: No memory without user control.
No sensitive data without locks.
No export without explicit user action.
No destructive action without explicit confirmation.
No Carnos destructive action.
No Carnos silent memory save.
No Carnos connector action without permission.
No raw token exposure.
No private data leakage into dashboards.
No private data leakage into analytics.
No locked custom tracker exposure.
No forgotten memory in Carnos context.
No Spotify action without connector permission.
No Spotify private-mode bypass.
No connector scope change without audit.
No audit log tampering.
No privacy action without audit trail.
No Spotify account connection without user OAuth.
No Spotify token access by Carnos.
No Spotify listening history memory without approval.
